page: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. This configuration provides 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with 2 threads per core) and a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core (2206.0) and multi-core (21074.0) metrics, as well as software compilation with Clang (59767.0) and image processing (21890.0). Static web serving throughput reaches 107.02 GB/sec, also placing in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is robust, with read-only small block cached speeds at 5103.56 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 197.26 GB/sec. For LLM inference, the processor's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support prompt processing of a medium 7B model at 306.67 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.16xlarge offers high compute density and advanced instruction sets (such as AVX-512 and AMX) without the overhead of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a resource-efficient choice for CPU-bound workloads that do not require persistent local NVMe drives. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive databases. The instance is best suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, image and text processing, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance has no local storage or GPUs, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark results place the c8i.16xlarge in the top tier for Geekbench single-core and multi-core performance, software compilation, and static web serving throughput. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and LLM inference capabilities, supported by hardware-level AVX-512 and AMX extensions. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks such as software development, web serving, and text processing, though its low memory-to-vCPU ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 3.9 GHz processor, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core).
Top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and static web serving.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for AI inference.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU hardware.
Best suited for compute-intensive workloads, web serving, and CPU-based inference.
